summary.en: Christensen's canonical low-end disruption case (1970s–1990s). Nucor's electric-arc mini-mills entered the steel industry from the bottom — rebar, the lowest-margin product — using lower-quality steel that integrated giants (US Steel, Bethlehem) were happy to exit. Generation by generation, Nucor improved metallurgy, attacked angle iron (1975), structural beams (1980s), and finally flat-rolled sheet steel for automakers (Crawfordsville thin-slab plant, 1989). Each retreat by the incumbents was 'rational' — defending higher-margin segments — until there was nothing left to retreat to. By 2001 Bethlehem Steel was bankrupt; Nucor became the largest US steel producer. Cited in Christensen (1997) Ch. 4 and 7 as the textbook example of how upmarket migration by a low-end disruptor proceeds.
summary.zh: Christensen 在《创新者的窘境》中给出的低端颠覆经典案例（1970s–1990s）。纽柯的电弧炉小型钢厂从行业最底部切入——利润最低的螺纹钢——用初期质量较差的钢材，让一体化巨头（US Steel、Bethlehem）「理性地」退出该段。一代又一代，纽柯改进冶金工艺、攻入角钢（1975）、结构梁（1980s），最终在 1989 年克劳福兹维尔薄板坯连铸厂打入汽车板这一最高利润段。在位者每一次撤退都是「理性的」——守住更高利润段——直到无段可守。2001 年伯利恒钢铁破产；纽柯成为美国最大钢铁生产商。Christensen 1997 第 4、7 章作为低端颠覆向上迁移的教科书案例引用。

sources.label: Christensen, C. M. · The Innovator's Dilemma · Harvard Business School Press · 1997 · Ch. 4, 7
Christensen, C. M. & Raynor, M. E. · The Innovator's Solution · HBS Press · 2003 · Ch. 2 (mini-mill upmarket migration)
Christensen, Raynor, McDonald · 'What Is Disruptive Innovation?' · HBR · December 2015
Iverson, F. K. · Plain Talk: Lessons from a Business Maverick · Wiley · 1997 (Nucor CEO memoir)
